notes: Tests whether skill enforces JTBD canonical vocabulary (hire/fire, progress in circumstances, functional/emotional/social dimensions) vs baseline generic product-discovery advice.

universal_assertions.text: Uses 'hire' and/or 'fire' language to describe how customers choose or abandon products
Names or clearly invokes the three dimensions of a job (functional, emotional, social)
Frames the job in terms of circumstances/context, not customer demographics or attributes

evals.name: churn-analysis
evals.prompt: A SaaS client tells me their users keep cancelling after 2 months. They've tried lower pricing, more onboarding emails, and a referral program. Nothing worked. Help me diagnose what's going on.

fixture_assertions.text: Reframes the problem as a job-mismatch or competing-solution problem, not a pricing/marketing problem
Asks what users are switching TO (what is getting hired in place of this product)

evals.name: videography-job-discovery
evals.prompt: What is the 'job' that a nonprofit ED hires a videographer to do when they commission a brand video? I'm trying to figure out what I'm really being paid for.

fixture_assertions.text: Explicitly moves beyond the functional job ('produce a video') to emotional/social dimensions
Describes the job as progress from one state to another in specific circumstances, not as a deliverable
